currentUiPhase.label: Phase 3.5 - Delivery-rich reader experience with partially productized interaction and tooling-heavy author surfaces
rationale: Reader delivery surfaces are broad and coherent across chapters/scenes/characters/places/timeline/symbols.
Interactive conversation stack is service-mature and strongly verified but UI remains debug-oriented.
Author/debug capabilities are substantial in backend/actions/tests, but several remain unwired to first-class UI.

strengths: Comprehensive scene reading surface with mode/audio/personalization hooks.
Strong contract and verification backbone around reader cockpit and interaction boundaries.
gaps: Continuity logic split between localStorage and server reader state.
Story reentry continuity is not surfaced in reader UX.
Moderation/degraded/fallback states are better represented in cockpit than in the public scene UX.

evidence: app/read/* routes mapped and build successfully

majorGaps.details: story-reentry, author-inspection, and conversation-observer actions/services exist without mapped product surfaces.
return/continue UX currently uses both localStorage and prisma.readerState paths.
AdminNav omits some existing routes (for example narrative hub and observer paths).

majorGaps.area: Runtime schema strictness for nested cockpit payload fields
majorGaps.details: Several nested readerCockpitPayload regions are validated as z.unknown() at registry level.

blockers: Define one authoritative continuity path for UI return/resume behavior.
Surface story reentry continuity in reader UX or explicitly retire unused action path.
Attach or explicitly scope author/debug action-only capabilities to avoid shadow UX.
nonBlockingFollowUps: Tighten cockpit payload nested runtime schemas where currently unknown.
Add focused page/component verification for public reader routes.
